g.text: Byte Offset | Member Variable | Size | Type

ol.li:
Two-Way Translation:
Inbound: Syscalls from process use id_map to check permissions against kernel kuid_t.
Outbound: Userland tools (e.g., ls) map kuid_t back to VUIDs via from_kuid().
Capability Scoping: Administrative actions (CAP_SYS_ADMIN) held by UID 0 inside a namespace are restricted to that namespace and its children.

Isolation Guarantee:
Real Root (Host UID 0) is never mapped to a standard container user.
Result: If a process escapes, it retains UID 1000 on the host, preventing host-level root compromise.

g.title: Process believes it is root within the namespace
The ID the kernel uses for permission checks
